description: Directive tamper proof (v0.4, §9.7): the agent reconstructs inbound_signed_context from the directive it RECEIVED (recomputing payload_sha256 and re-reading the bound from/id/to). The honest directive reproduces the signed context and verifies; a directive whose `to` is redirected to another agent (or whose body/from is altered) diverges the context, so verification MUST fail with a signature mismatch — a directive signed for one agent cannot be replayed into another's mailbox.
obligation: Honest directive verifies against v1; a tampered `to`/`from`/`body` fails with `signature mismatch` (not an incidental window/key/jti rejection).

honest_directive.title: Pause deploys until the incident is resolved
honest_directive.body: We are mid-incident on prod. Hold all deploys until I say otherwise.

tampered_directive.title: Pause deploys until the incident is resolved
tampered_directive.body: We are mid-incident on prod. Hold all deploys until I say otherwise.
